channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India Cuts E2W EV Subsidies, Adds Performance Rules
item.description: India's government will keep incentives for electric two-wheelers (E2Ws) until July 31, 2026, through the PM E-DRIVE scheme. However, subsidy amounts are being cut, and new performance standards will be required for EVs starting in January 2026. This signals a move away from strong government support towards a market where companies need to compete more on product innovation and price.

item.title: HDFC Bank's Governance Crisis Fuels ICICI Bank's Climb
item.description: HDFC Bank is facing challenges from a governance probe after its chairman resigned and from strains following its merger with HDFC Ltd., including a high loan-to-deposit ratio and squeezed profit margins. This places HDFC Bank in a difficult 're-building' phase. In contrast, rival ICICI Bank shows stronger asset quality, faster growth, and healthier profits, earning it higher market valuations. ICICI benefits from a stable operating environment and is seen as being in a 'sustenance' mode.

item.title: Premier Energies Adds 5.6 GW Solar Plant, Total Capacity Hits 11.1 GW
item.description: Premier Energies has launched a new 5.6 GW solar module manufacturing plant in Telangana, boosting its total capacity to 11.1 GW. This expansion is part of a ₹12,500 crore investment strategy focused on integrated solar production, including backward integration into ingots and wafers, and expanding into components like inverters and transformers. The company is also adopting advanced 0BB TOPCon cell technology, strengthening its position in India's fast-growing solar market.

item.title: Paras Health Aims for 3,000 Beds in North India Expansion Ahead of IPO
item.description: Paras Health plans to increase its North India bed capacity by about 800 beds over three years to reach nearly 3,000. The expansion includes new facilities in Gurugram and Ludhiana, plus upgrades at existing sites. The company is self-funding this growth, positioning itself for a potential IPO.

item.title: India's 2026 Tax Overhaul: Bigger Allowances, Stricter Disclosures
item.description: India's Income-tax Act 2025, starting April 1, 2026, offers more tax-free benefits like higher allowances for rent, education, meals, and gifts. However, it also requires stricter details for deductions and replaces the old Form 16 with a new Form 130 for TDS. The overhaul aims to benefit taxpayers while improving transparency and modernizing tax collection.

item.title: India's Diesel Exports Hit 7-Year High Amid Geopolitical Boom, Taxes Loom
item.description: India has achieved its highest diesel exports in seven years, primarily propelled by Reliance Industries leveraging high Asian refining margins amid geopolitical turmoil. This surge occurred as key suppliers like China restricted exports, forcing regional buyers to seek alternatives. However, India's recent imposition of export duties on diesel and evolving global supply dynamics cast a shadow over the sustainability of these lucrative trade flows and refiner profitability.

item.title: Eutelsat Pursues ISRO for Satellite Launches to Cut Costs, Boost Options
item.description: Eutelsat Communications is in strategic discussions with India's space agency (ISRO) to secure launch services, marking a significant effort to diversify beyond established providers like SpaceX and Arianespace. This move is driven by the need for cost-efficient launch capacity as Eutelsat expands its satellite fleet and faces escalating competition in the global broadband market from major players like Starlink and Amazon's Project Kuiper. The talks align with India's push to commercialize its space sector and position ISRO's LVM3 rocket as a competitive global offering.

item.title: Hashdex Crypto ETF NCIQ Adds Options for Institutional Risk Tools
item.description: Hashdex Nasdaq CME Crypto Index ETF (NCIQ) has introduced options trading, a key step for institutional investors seeking advanced risk management for diversified digital asset exposure. The move enables hedging, income generation, and structured product creation, giving crypto ETFs more traditional finance features. Despite recent crypto market declines, this launch signals a maturing investment landscape for digital assets.

item.title: India Cancels Critical Mineral Auctions Over Low Investor Interest
item.description: India's government canceled auctions for 11 critical mineral blocks, citing a lack of investor interest. This is a setback for the country's plans to increase domestic production of essential resources like lithium and rare earth elements, crucial for economic growth and the clean energy transition. The cancellations highlight issues with market appeal, geological data, or regulations, raising concerns about supply chain security.

item.title: India Boosts Russian Oil Amid Mideast Tensions, Shipping Costs Soar
item.description: India's crude oil imports hit a nine-month high in March, with a significant increase from Russia due to Middle East supply disruptions. The country is also turning to Angola for alternatives. However, India faces soaring shipping costs and increased geopolitical risks from key chokepoints like the Strait of Hormuz. This shift is vital for supply but raises economic pressures, including inflation and currency drops, as global oil prices climb above $100 a barrel.

item.title: India Home Loans Face Affordability Squeeze Amid Surging Property Prices
item.description: Despite the Reserve Bank of India keeping its repo rate steady at 5.25%, leading to stable home loan interest rates for March 2026, the Indian housing market faces a significant affordability crisis. Floating rates remain between 7.10% and 8.50%, while fixed rates come at a premium. This forces borrowers to balance the cost of payment certainty against potential savings. The situation is worsened by rapidly increasing property values and a rising household debt-to-GDP ratio, making homeownership a challenge for mid-income buyers.

item.title: Huawei India Gets Tax Win, But Audit Scrutiny Continues
item.description: The Delhi High Court has partially granted relief to Huawei Telecommunications (India) concerning income tax proceedings. The court quashed reassessment notices and special audit directions for the financial year 2013-14. However, it upheld reassessment proceedings and special audit orders for the financial year 2015-16, indicating continued tax scrutiny for that period. This bifurcated ruling reflects the complex legal and financial environment Huawei India operates within.

item.title: India's Mineral Auctions Flop: Low Investor Interest Halts Resource Push
item.description: India's sixth round of critical and strategic mineral block auctions has been drastically reduced, with 11 blocks cancelled due to a significant lack of investor interest and qualified bidders. This hinders the nation's strategic goals to boost domestic exploration and production of minerals vital for economic development and national security, especially as global supply chains face volatility. The cancellations highlight ongoing challenges in attracting participation, with previous auction rounds also facing similar setbacks. This pattern suggests deeper issues affecting the country's ambition to secure resources for clean energy and advanced technologies.

item.title: India Housing Sales Plunge 13% on Supply Shock; Key Cities Split
item.description: India's residential property market experienced a sharp contraction in Q1 2026, with sales across the top nine cities falling to 98,761 units, the lowest in 18 quarters. This downturn is primarily attributed to a significant 19% year-on-year decline in new project launches, which restricted overall absorption. While Bengaluru and Delhi-NCR demonstrated resilience, most other markets, particularly in the West, saw demand and supply shrink concurrently, showing a divided market recovery. The real estate sector's benchmark index, Nifty Realty, has also suffered substantial losses year-to-date, showing investor worries.

item.title: Welspun Corp Target Lifted to Rs 1,082; Stock Faces Profit Pressure
item.description: Nuvama International Equities upgraded Welspun Corp to a Rs 1,082 target, citing a strong Rs 24,700 crore order book and strategic diversification. While revenue grew in Q3 FY26, net profit fell 33% year-over-year. The stock is down 4.5% over 12 months, and its ability to convert backlog into steady profits amid tough market conditions remains a key focus.

item.title: Indian Retail Investors Cut Cash, Flood Derivatives Despite Losses
item.description: India's retail investors are showing a split strategy: slashing cash market investment value for FY26, signaling caution over valuations and global risks. Meanwhile, they remain heavily engaged in equity derivatives, drawn by leverage and digital access. Despite significant losses, especially in derivatives, and tighter regulations, traders are opting for riskier instruments amid heightened market volatility from geopolitical events.

item.title: Sathya Agencies IPO: ₹600 Cr Raise Amid Thin Profit Margins
item.description: Sathya Agencies is seeking ₹600 crore through an IPO, allocating ₹175 crore for Unilet Appliances acquisition and ₹35 crore for debt reduction. The retailer posted ₹3,496 crore revenue but a modest ₹46 crore profit in FY25, operating 392 stores. The offering comes amid a cautious IPO market and intense competition in India's growing consumer electronics sector, raising questions about margin sustainability.

item.title: IndiQube Secures ₹75 Cr Bengaluru Workspace Deal with Healthcare Tech GCC
item.description: IndiQube Spaces has secured a significant Rs 75 crore workspace leasing deal in Bengaluru with a major healthcare technology Global Capability Centre. The agreement spans over 48,000 sq. ft. along the prime Outer Ring Road, highlighting strong demand from GCCs for flexible, high-quality office solutions. This comes as IndiQube reports a robust 45% year-on-year revenue growth for Q3 FY26.

item.title: NBFCs Rush for Funds at Higher Rates as Corporates Conserve Cash
item.description: In March 2026, Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) sharply increased commercial paper (CP) issuances by nearly 55% to ₹70,300 crore, driven by year-end funding needs despite higher borrowing costs. In contrast, corporate CP issuances fell 35% to ₹26,600 crore as companies prioritized internal cash and cost savings. While the banking sector's Certificates of Deposit (CD) market remained stable, overall system liquidity tightened, pushing short-term rates higher and straining bank margins due to slower deposit growth.

item.title: India's Finances Hold Strong Amid Energy Shocks, Rupee Drop
item.description: India's financial system is navigating global energy shocks and rupee depreciation with remarkable resilience. Despite rising Brent crude and a falling currency, the nation enters this challenging period with significantly stronger buffers, deeper markets, and a more adaptive financial architecture than in previous decades, indicating robust underlying economic strength.

item.title: Keyrock Valued at $1.1 Billion, Achieves Unicorn Status
item.description: Keyrock has reached $1.1 billion valuation and achieved unicorn status after its Series C funding round, led by SC Ventures. The company raised up to $100 million to strengthen its finances, grow services, and acquire businesses. This investment highlights increasing institutional trust in digital assets and Keyrock's role connecting traditional finance with crypto, boosted by its recent acquisition of Turing Capital.

item.title: SIS Group Posts 24.5% Revenue Growth, But Q3 Shows Net Loss on Costs
item.description: SIS Group's Q3 FY26 saw revenue surge by 24.5% to ₹4,185 crore, fueled by its strategic pivot towards AI, robotics, and an opex service model. However, exceptional expenses of ₹290 crore linked to new Labour Codes resulted in a net loss of ₹138.37 crore. The company is investing over ₹100 crore in technology and integrating its acquisition of APS Group, aiming for a $4 billion platform by 2030, even as operational costs temporarily impact profitability.

item.title: Signature Global Stock Dives 3.9% Amid Valuation Concerns After JV
item.description: Signature Global India Limited's stock fell 3.91% to ₹714.20 on March 30, 2026, nearing a 52-week low. The drop followed a 50:50 joint venture for subsidiary Gurugram Commercity Limited (GCL) with Millennia Realtors, diluting Signature Global's control. Investors reacted negatively to the deal, citing concerns over the company's high valuation and financial performance, despite positive analyst ratings.

item.title: Texmaco Rail Wins ₹357 Cr Order, Stock Falls Amid Market Concerns
item.description: Texmaco Rail & Engineering secured a ₹357.11 crore order from JSW Group for railway wagons and rakes. Despite the boost to its order book, the company's stock fell 5.69% on Monday, trading near its 52-week low. This follows a significant drop in Q3 FY26 profit and revenue, though analysts still recommend buying.

item.title: India Credit Cards Slow Down as New Rules Push Profit Focus
item.description: Credit card spending growth in India slowed to 11% in FY26, a marked deceleration from 15% previously. Regulatory actions, a strategic pivot away from rent payment processing, and competition from UPI are pressuring traditional revenue streams. Issuance of new cards has fallen to its lowest since the pandemic, prompting a shift towards deepening engagement with existing customers and a bifurcation of card offerings into premium and basic segments to drive profitability.

item.title: Dish TV's Legal Fight: Free TV vs. Pay DTH Survival
item.description: Dish TV India has petitioned the Kerala High Court to bring Prasar Bharati's DD Free Dish under the same regulatory framework as private DTH operators. The company argues DD Free Dish's unencrypted, free-to-air model creates a discriminatory, unsustainable competitive advantage. This legal battle highlights a critical juncture for India's pay DTH market, which has seen a significant subscriber decline from 69.6 million in FY21 to below 51 million by early FY26, while DD Free Dish approaches 53 million homes. DD Free Dish's revenue from slot auctions has surged, further intensifying the disparity for heavily invested private operators.

item.title: West Asia Conflict Risks India's Growth and Inflation: EY Report
item.description: The West Asia conflict poses a major economic risk to India, potentially lowering FY27 GDP growth by up to 1 percentage point and raising inflation by 1.5 percentage points from baseline forecasts, an EY report states. India's heavy reliance on energy imports makes it vulnerable, impacting sectors that create jobs and requiring policy responses. Analyst forecasts for FY27 GDP growth vary widely, from 5.9% to 7.1%, reflecting economic uncertainty.

item.title: We Win Ltd Stock Drops 5.5% Despite Rs 14 Crore Contract Win
item.description: We Win Ltd announced a Rs 14.11 crore work order from the National Health Mission, Madhya Pradesh, for ICCC and district helpdesks. Despite this significant contract, the company's stock fell 5.55% on Monday, trading near its 52-week low. The drop signals investor worries about the micro-cap firm's ability to execute projects, especially with wider market issues affecting smaller companies.

item.title: NTPC Green Energy Seals Ammonia Deal Amid Stock Dip
item.description: NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd (NGEL) has agreed to supply 70,000 metric tonnes of green ammonia annually to Krishna Phoschem Limited via SECI. This deal supports India's National Green Hydrogen Mission. Despite the strategic move, NTPC Green Energy's stock fell 2.69% to ₹92.50 on Monday, indicating investor caution.

item.title: Middle East Conflict Risks 1% Hit to India GDP Growth
item.description: The Middle East conflict could reduce India's GDP growth by 1% and increase inflation by 1.5%. Sectors like textiles, paints, and fertilizers face direct impacts, potentially hurting demand. India's reliance on imports for oil, gas, and fertilizers makes it vulnerable.

item.title: Gravita India Expands Copper Business with RMIL Acquisition
item.description: Gravita India Limited has finalized the acquisition of 98.95% of Rashtriya Metal Industries Limited (RMIL) for ₹559.08 crore, marking a significant strategic entry into the copper recycling and manufacturing domain. This move is poised to leverage the growing demand for copper in sectors like electric vehicles and renewable energy, augmenting Gravita's existing multi-metal recycling portfolio.

item.title: Manipal Hospitals Files ₹8,000 Crore IPO to Cut Debt, Fund Growth
item.description: Manipal Health Enterprises filed for an ₹8,000 crore IPO. The company plans to use the funds mainly to refinance debt and make a strategic acquisition. This aims to strengthen its financial position and support expansion in India's growing healthcare market, even as the IPO market becomes more selective.

item.title: Indian IT Earnings: AI Disruption Puts Focus on FY27 Outlook, Rupee
item.description: Indian IT firms are set to report Q4FY26 results, facing AI disruption and global uncertainty. While IT stocks outperformed in January, they underperformed in February and March. Investors are now prioritizing FY27 revenue guidance and order books over immediate Q4 performance. Rupee depreciation offers a marginal benefit to margins. Valuations remain pressured, with a strong rerating unlikely if Generative AI fears persist.

item.title: Black Box Stock Falls After ₹386 Crore Fundraise: Valuation Worries Remain
item.description: Black Box Ltd secured ₹386.36 crore through warrant conversion, with promoters increasing their stake to 69.99%. Despite the capital boost, the stock fell 3.16% on Friday. This happened even as the IT sector grows; Black Box's P/E ratio of 35-40x is higher than some rivals, and analyst price targets vary widely.

item.title: Jyoti Structures Stock Drops 7% Despite Rs 88 Crore Adani Order
item.description: Jyoti Structures secured an Rs 88.33 crore transmission infrastructure order from Adani Electricity Mumbai for the Coastal Road Project. However, the stock fell 6.83% on Monday, indicating investor concerns over the company's execution and financial health despite the significant win.

item.title: Supply Crisis Puts IT & Pharma Valuations Under Pressure
item.description: Markets face a unique crisis from actual supply disruptions, making traditional tools less effective. This has led to a defensive shift towards Information Technology and Pharmaceuticals. However, ongoing supply issues could raise costs for IT firms and raw materials for pharma, while high pharma valuations need careful review. Multi-Commodity Exchange (MCX) benefits from volatility but risks demand destruction. Analysts are mostly positive on IT and MCX, but urge caution on high-priced pharma stocks.

item.title: Zomato, Swiggy Hike Delivery Fees Aiming for Profit Amid Tough Competition
item.description: Food delivery leaders Zomato and Swiggy are raising platform fees to boost profitability in a stable duopoly market. This strategic shift aims to offset investments, but strong competition, especially in quick commerce, and evolving consumer habits create challenges. Analysts have mixed views, with some seeing turnaround potential and others warning of market share loss and valuation risks. Traditional retailers like DMart, Titan, and Trent currently show stronger valuation metrics compared to their past.

item.title: Coforge Gets RBI OK for $2.35B Encora Acquisition on March 31
item.description: Coforge Ltd. achieved a significant regulatory milestone on March 31, 2026, receiving Reserve Bank of India approval for an overseas direct investment exceeding $1 billion, clearing a major hurdle for its $2.35 billion acquisition of Encora. This approval, crucial for the transaction, propels Coforge's strategy to bolster its AI, cloud, and data engineering capabilities. The move comes as the broader Indian IT sector navigates AI disruption fears and macro uncertainty, positioning Coforge's aggressive expansion against a challenging backdrop.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Credit's AAA Rating Affirmed Amid Auto Sector Concerns
item.description: CARE Ratings has reaffirmed an 'AAA' rating with a 'Stable' outlook for Bajaj Auto Credit Limited's long-term bank facilities totaling ₹10,000 crore. This comes as Bajaj Auto Limited faces brokerage concerns regarding its three-wheeler segment exposure in Maharashtra and potential supply chain disruptions and commodity price hikes linked to West Asia tensions. The rating provides a stable financial footing for the subsidiary despite external sector risks.

item.title: India's IRDAI Mandates Health Insurance Overhaul: Simple Policies, New Scores
item.description: Starting June 2026, India's IRDAI is overhauling health insurance with simple, low-cost policies and new performance scorecards for insurers and hospitals. These reforms aim to improve transparency and affordability in a sector facing profit pressures, high claim payouts (₹85,000 crore last year), patient complaints, and low market penetration.
